title: Wisdom And Prudence.
content:
Wouldst thou, my friend, mount up to the highest summit of wisdom,
    Be not deterred by the fear, prudence thy course may deride
    That shortsighted one sees but the bank that from thee is flying,
    Not the one which ere long thou wilt attain with bold flight.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Friedrich Schiller, titled "Wisdom And Prudence.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
